cards.title: Buyer Rescue Workflow
cards.problem: A buyer cannot access a purchased file or needs help after checkout. A normal AI response may be polite but incomplete; it may miss access-state classification, refund boundaries, escalation logic, or a public-safe proof note.
cards.mission: Convert a recurring support scenario into a reusable GoalOS workflow: classify the issue, generate a safe response, flag human review when needed, preserve private buyer data off-chain, and produce a Proof Card showing what improved.
cards.incremental: Establishes the first proof surface: AI work becomes an auditable workflow improvement rather than a disposable answer.
cards.value: Faster support, clearer buyer experience, fewer repeated mistakes, and the first public demonstration that proof-backed workflow improvement can be understood by non-technical buyers.
plans: Buyer Rescue Workflow proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room
cards.problem: A serious buyer asks whether the system can be evaluated, audited, and trusted. A generic sales page is not enough for procurement, compliance, or institutional review.
cards.mission: Turn procurement due diligence into a proof mission: assemble a trust room with public-safe evidence, claim boundaries, security posture, token-utility explanation, Proof Cards, reviewer notes, and an Evidence Docket checklist.
cards.incremental: Moves from buyer support to institutional trust: proof becomes useful in procurement conversations.
cards.value: Better procurement readiness, faster sponsor conversations, clearer risk posture, and reusable answers for future enterprise buyers.
plans: Sovereign Procurement Trust Room proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign AI Procurement Control Tower
cards.problem: Enterprise AI adoption stalls when buyers cannot see governance, review, rollback, evidence, and improvement history in one place.
cards.mission: Build a procurement control tower that connects proof cards, Evidence Dockets, reviewer decisions, rollout status, rollback paths, credentials, and reputation into one decision surface.
cards.incremental: Adds operating control: the buyer sees not just trust artifacts, but a governance route for decisions.
cards.value: Higher buyer confidence, clearer executive approval pathway, better risk control, and a stronger corporate sales motion.
plans: Sovereign AI Procurement Control Tower proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine
cards.problem: One-off AI tasks do not compound. They answer, then disappear. A persistent system must accumulate capabilities, evidence, and value over time.
cards.mission: Define the accumulation layer: every accepted workflow improvement becomes an artifact; every artifact receives evidence; every evidence-backed artifact can earn credentials, reputation, and future routing rights.
cards.incremental: Moves from individual proof cards to compounding institutional memory.
cards.value: Capabilities stop being ephemeral and become reusable operating assets that improve future missions.
plans: Persistent Goal-Seeking Intelligence Accumulation Engine proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign RSI Value-to-Capability Treasury
cards.problem: Accumulated value must not remain inert. It must be governed, budgeted, and reinvested into stronger future capabilities.
cards.mission: Turn verified value into governed capability budgets: successful proof missions produce value signals; value is routed through treasury controls; stronger future proof missions receive budget, talent, tools, and review capacity.
cards.incremental: Adds economic recursion: verified value funds stronger future work.
cards.value: Connects proof to reinvestment, allowing the system to improve its own operating capacity without relying only on manual planning.
plans: Sovereign RSI Value-to-Capability Treasury proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System
cards.problem: A sovereign enterprise needs more than isolated tools. It needs a multi-agent operating system with aim, strategy, execution, proof, risk control, settlement, rollback, and memory.
cards.mission: Coordinate specialist agent roles—Aim Council, Strategy Desk, Builders, Proof Engineers, Reviewers, Red Team, Treasury, Rollback Desk, and Evidence Office—through proof-backed workflows and AGIALPHA settlement.
cards.incremental: Turns the proof flywheel into an enterprise operating system.
cards.value: Creates the institutional form needed for scalable, accountable, multi-agent enterprise execution.
plans: Sovereign Enterprise RSI Operating System proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign Enterprise Autoresearch Reactor
cards.problem: Automated discovery is useful only when the system can generate hypotheses, test them, validate them, reject failures, combine branches, and remember what worked.
cards.mission: Run parallel enterprise improvement threads: Domain Scouts identify opportunities, Hypothesis Engineers propose interventions, Builder Cells execute, Evaluator Operators test, Reviewers validate, Red Teams falsify, and the Selection Gate promotes only proven improvements.
cards.incremental: Adds autonomous discovery and experimental search to the enterprise operating system.
cards.value: Creates a repeatable machinery for finding improvements across corporate workflows, risk controls, security, procurement, compute, finance, and operations.
plans: Sovereign Enterprise Autoresearch Reactor proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Sovereign Corporate Capability Foundry
cards.problem: Enterprises need capability growth across multiple profitable domains, not only one research benchmark. The system must allocate missions across procurement, finance, security, operations, energy, compute, and R&D.
cards.mission: Create a portfolio-level capability foundry that assigns proof missions to specialist cells, measures alpha-Work Units, validates output, issues proof cards, and reinvests verified value across domains.
cards.incremental: Moves from autoresearch to portfolio-level corporate capability formation.
cards.value: Shows how a sovereign corporation can systematically convert work into a growing library of reusable capabilities.
plans: Sovereign Corporate Capability Foundry proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Execution Moat
cards.problem: Cyber risk is a high-value sovereign corporate domain where unverified automation is dangerous. The system needs authorized scope, defensive boundaries, evidence, regression harnesses, red-team review, and rollback readiness.
cards.mission: Turn authorized cyber weakness remediation into proof-settled work: map assets, triage risk, build remediation candidates, run security regression harnesses, validate through reviewers and red teams, and promote only safe remediations.
cards.incremental: Moves the foundry into a high-value cyber-sovereign execution domain.
cards.value: Creates a defensible execution moat around trusted remediation, secure deployment velocity, supply-chain evidence, and reusable defensive capability.
plans: Cyber-Sovereign Execution Moat proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.

cards.title: Cyber-Sovereign Proof Benchmark Market
cards.problem: Architecture is not enough. A durable execution moat requires replayable benchmarks, independent reproduction, hardened evaluators, and public-safe evidence that buyers can inspect.
cards.mission: Build a cyber-sovereign proof benchmark market: authorized defensive tasks, baseline manifests, evaluator harnesses, candidate artifacts, ProofBundles, reviewer attestations, red-team falsification reports, reproduction receipts, and AGIALPHA settlement.
cards.incremental: Turns the cyber execution moat into a benchmark market where results can be replayed and compared.
cards.value: Creates market-legible proof: not merely a claim of improvement, but a reproducible path showing what improved, what it cost, what risks were controlled, and what may propagate.
plans: Cyber-Sovereign Proof Benchmark Market proof mission plan
cards.rsi: The accepted artifact may influence future work only after evidence, evaluator review, reviewer validation, scope control, challenge window, rollback readiness, Selection Gate approval, and Chronicle entry.
